Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Deterministic Optimization Proxies
Short Hypothesis: Optimizing deterministic optimization proxies through curvature-aware precision routing can enhance computational efficiency and numerical stability without requiring additional training.
Related Work: Existing methods like Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) focus on test-time optimization via low-rank subspace projection but do not address curvature-aware precision routing. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware techniques with deterministic optimization proxies, offering a novel approach to enhancing computational efficiency and numerical stability.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for optimizing deterministic optimization proxies through curvature-aware precision routing. This approach leverages geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies to enhance computational efficiency and numerical stability without requiring additional training. By integrating curvature-aware techniques with deterministic optimization proxies, we aim to provide a mathematically transparent and resource-efficient solution for optimizing machine learning models. Our framework prioritizes empirical validation and mechanistic transparency, ensuring rigorous theoretical alignment and feasibility for single-GPU deployments. We will validate our approach through systematic ablation protocols, multi-seed variance quantification, and formal PAC-Bayes verification.
Experiments: Conduct a systematic ablation study to evaluate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on computational efficiency and numerical stability.
Perform multi-seed variance quantification to assess the robustness of the proposed framework.
Validate the framework using formal PAC-Bayes verification to ensure theoretical alignment.
Compare the proposed framework with existing methods like Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) on standard benchmarks to demonstrate its advantages.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require careful tuning of curvature-aware parameters to achieve optimal performance.
Empirical validation on a single-GPU setup may limit the scalability of the framework.
The theoretical analysis may be complex, requiring significant mathematical expertise.
